I wonder if that would work better than just peddling to power the engine?

author: @hunnybunnysheavymetalmusic6542
text:
I think some people would be exhausted before they could charge their phone.

Also, the vanes would work better if you put a smooth narrow edged wiper along the outer edges [either Teflon or HDPE come to mind] and some lubricant such as silicone oil or grease. [need only be a molecule thin film]

replies.author: @hunnybunnysheavymetalmusic6542
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkeringSame amount of effort required, just a longer amount of travel to make it happen.

It would make more sense to me to use a hand crank generator.

It would be more compact and make more mechanical sense from a speed and efficiency standpoint.

I have one of those tiny little pocket hand crank lights [or 'torches' as they say in the UK] and its not even the size of a deck of cards.

I could readily charge a cell phone with this thing and its small enough to slip in my pocket.

I could even see making a charger like this specifically so that it snaps to and plugs into the back of one's cell phone as a passive power extender that one can crank any time they are bored or while they are walking from one location to another, even with it attached to their phones.

author: @tonycrankhead3634
text: When you say glued the paddles together, don't they have to slide independently? With the eccentricity, its neither a constant radius, nor a constant diameter.

replies.author: @tonycrankhead3634
replies.text: Hello Robert. At 2'40" in video, it is explained that the vanes are glued in pairs. That means the total length of that vane pair is fixed. It means that both ends of that vane pair cannot be in contact with the outer walls at all times.  Still works well, but there could be some leak around the ends. If all 4 vanes were separate, they would close those gaps...@ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: ah - thank you for expanding mate - you are absolutely right - I glued them as I was keeping the top off for demo purposes - but you are spot on - if you put springs in there that would help too - cheers mate

replies.author: @tonycrankhead3634
replies.text: My pleasure. I am working on my alternative patented vane pump technology, and power generation is one of my goals. When more advanced, perhaps I'll send you one. And thanks to you, I will be looking at serpentine windings...@ThinkingandTinkering

author: @porethoose
text: @Robertmurray-Smith  Greetings and salutations. I'm curious if you could help me. I'm currently trying to devise a way to make sewage flowing in a pipe create electricity. Either with wave generator style or standard pump/ water wheel designs. All contained in a high flow low volume secondary pipe. This pipe can be installed into existing sewers. Something that if the volume increases. It'll then release into the primary flow sewer. I'm inexperienced of the many different water flow generators. I mostly feel Tesla's turbine might be the best low impedance design. I'd like to hear your opinions of the subject. I'd prefer these ideas and designs to be public for academic and public well being. Not a monetary gain.

any ideas to make an bolt-on generator to attach to a bicycle??  (I should say a STATIONARY bicycle) Preferably one that attaches to the chain after removing the rear wheel and not on that relies on a friction/pressure fit against the tire... If an 10 or 18 speed is used then you could really get some massive power from it..... 
You see I don't have a 3D printer and I can't really save up money to get one right now, so I've got a thousand ideas in my head and your videos are right up my alley!! SO that's why I've been making suggestions :)

replies.author: @TerrorTubbie666

author: @affiliatereviews4079
text: You need a minimum of three bellows/pistons to make your turbocharger work. Three, because that is the minimum to have the power pulses overlap enough to keep the vane motor or impeller spinning from the exhaust gases. That's the math behind achates power's opposed piston engine which uses both turbo and super charging to make the engine work. For your education,  dear reader.

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
Beautiful way to help someone understand the system as just a collection of modules.
Something to take energy from one source (your arm's oscillating linear motion),
Turn it into another kind (airflow/pressure differential),
Control where it goes (one-way valve),
Do it again (airflow into rotary motion),
Then one more time (rotary to electrical potential)
Again, just beautiful.
